attacking_style: Opportunistic long-range solo attacks from breakaways, often with repeated accelerations in final 1-6km. Willing to gamble on early moves for strategic gains (KOM points, stage hunting) rather than waiting for finish.
team_tactics: Operates with minimal team support; self-sufficient breakaway rider who relies on equipment innovation (62t chainrings) and individual power rather than domestique positioning. Tactical intelligence to recognize when gaps stabilize.
terrain_preference: Flat to rolling stages and time trials where pure power dominates. Excels on descents with extreme gearing. Struggles visibly on climbs—even Cat 4 efforts compromise him on undulating terrain.
vulnerability: Grand Tour accumulation severely degrades TT specialist power by week three (beaten by Haga despite European champion status). Climbing weakness exploited on any sustained or repeated gradients; burns matches too early when attempting repeated attacks on climbs.
key_patterns: Time trial specialist whose power fades significantly in third week of Grand Tours (2019 Giro evidence).
Uses equipment innovation strategically: 62t chainring gambles, extreme gearing choices on descents (Dwars 2022).
Repeated attacking style within final 6km from breakaways rather than single decisive move (multiple Giro stages).
Visible suffering on even modest climbs (Cat 4) despite being in breakaways, not pure puncheur.
Converts breakaway opportunities to wins via sprint after trading attacks, not pure solo power (Giro 2021 win).
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 observations show visible suffering on minor climbs even in breakaways, suggesting declining climbing tolerance or poor Tour form compared to earlier aggressive classics/Giro performances
